overview: Rakdos Goblins is a Legacy aggro deck that relies on cheating creatures into play early using Aether Vial and Goblin Lackey. It aims to overwhelm opponents with a wide board of hasty Goblins, using toolbox cards like Goblin Matron and removal like Broadside Bombardiers to control the game. The deck wins by racing opponents with combat damage or draining life with Sling-Gang Lieutenant.
win_conditions: Swarming the board with hasty Goblins to deal combat damage
Using Sling-Gang Lieutenant to drain life
Casting Muxus, Goblin Grandee for a massive threat

keep_criteria: Have Aether Vial or Goblin Lackey in hand
Have a nice sequence for the first three turns
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (duals/lands that fetch), Goblin Lackey, Food Chain, or a 2-drop (Piledriver/Chieftain/Rabblemaster).
Prioritize hands that can execute a Turn 1 Lackey into a Turn 2 free cast.
Against control-heavy fields, keep hands with Disfigure or Hexing Squelcher.
mulligan_criteria: Lack Aether Vial or Goblin Lackey without a strong early sequence
Hand cannot impact turn 1 or 2 effectively
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no combo pieces or threats, or hands with only 1 land and no early plays.

examples.reason: Doesn't do anything on turn 1, can put a 2/2 on turn 2, and Muxus won't go in play soon.
Can play uncounterable Lackey on turn 1 and Matron on turn 2, which can find Muxus.
Can put Lackey in play on turn 1, might put Muxus on turn 2, and has removals for blockers.
Two Vials on turn 1 means you'll basically be able to resolve at least 1, even against Force of Will.

early_game: Play Aether Vial or Goblin Lackey on turn 1 to establish early board presence
Use 1-drops like Greasewrench Goblin to filter lands or play aggressively
Turn 1: Play land, cast Goblin Lackey.
Turn 2: Play land, cast Food Chain, sacrifice Lackey to cast Goblin Piledriver or Chieftain.
If you have a pump spell or Rabblemaster, hold it for Turn 2 or 3 to maximize value.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast the free spell on Turn 2.
If you draw a 2-drop in hand, you can cast it for free on Turn 2.
mid_game: Use Munitions Expert and Broadside Bombardiers to control the board
Utilize Goblin Matron as a toolbox to find answers or threats
Refill resources with Rundvelt Hordemaster, Greasewrench Goblin, and Goblin Ringleader
Do not overcommit to Food Chain without a target or protection.
Use Hexing Squelcher to protect key threats or recur Goblin Welder.
Manage your graveyard carefully against hate, but generally keep it stocked with Lackey/Chain/Welder.
do not untap with excess mana if you can cast a threat or cast Hexing Squelcher for {1}.
Commit threats when you have a way to protect them or when the board is clear.
late_game: Give haste to your horde with Howlsquad Heavy or Draconautics Engineer
Use Sling-Gang Lieutenant to drain life and finish the game
Use Gempalm Incinerator for extra removal and card draw in drawn-out matches
Goblin Piledriver with Rabblemaster/Bomb, Scuttling Doom Engine, or a swarm of goblins with pump spells.
Get there by chaining Lackey/Chain, then using Welder/Squelcher to recur threats, or pumping Rabblemaster to lethal.
When you have a clear path to lethal, the opponent has no sweepers, or you're ahead in the race.
Go all-in with pump spells and bombs.
If the opponent has no interaction left, commit your entire hand and board to close the game.

key_cards.name: Aether Vial
key_cards.notes: Essential for turn 1 plays; having two increases chances of resolving one against counterspells.

key_cards.name: Goblin Lackey
key_cards.notes: Provides uncounterable turn 1 plays and changes how the deck performs entirely.

key_cards.name: Muxus, Goblin Grandee
key_cards.notes: An extreme way to put Goblins in play; a massive threat that opponents struggle to handle.

key_cards.name: Goblin Matron
key_cards.notes: Provides various answers and can find Muxus, Goblin Grandee if needed.

key_cards.name: Broadside Bombardiers
key_cards.notes: Controls the board alongside Munitions Expert; crucial for going over threats like Atraxa, Grand Unifier.

key_cards.name: Howlsquad Heavy
key_cards.notes: Combines Goblin Rabblemaster and Goblin Warchief; grows the board constantly and gives haste.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize resolving Aether Vial or Goblin Lackey on turn 1 to set the pace
Use Greasewrench Goblin's ability to filter lands or unused Aether Vials when needed
Save Stingscourger to bounce big threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn back to hand
Create gigantic boards and sacrifice them instantly with Sling-Gang Lieutenant to kill opponents on their turn against The One Ring
Common mistakes: Overcommitting to Food Chain without a target, forgetting to protect your combo pieces, mismanaging the graveyard, not tracking opponent's hand size.
Unique interactions: Hexing Squelcher can protect Goblin Lackey or Food Chain from removal.
Scuttling Doom Engine can recur Goblin Welder for infinite value if you have enough goblins.
Remember that Goblin Piledriver's power is based on goblins you control, not just the ones you cast.
Always be ready to pivot to an aggressive plan if your combo is disrupted.

matchups.advice: Bring Leyline of the Void and Blasts. Use Broadside Bombardiers to go over Atraxa, Grand Unifier. Stingscourger can turn the tables. Howlsquad Heavy and Munitions Expert can be liabilities against reanimated threats.
Aether Vial can win on its own if resolved. Use answers for small threats and Murktide Regent. Once you gain board advantage, they lack tools to revert it.
Vial and Lackey go over mana disruption. Cheat out Muxus, Goblin Grandee. Sideboard plan involves removing random Goblins, adding removals, and a Moon effect.
Fury balances the matchup. Lackey can ignore Chalice of the Void with Cavern of Souls. Create gigantic boards and sacrifice with Sling-Gang Lieutenant to bypass The One Ring.
Difficult matchup. Sideboard into pseudo-control to prevent Nadu advantage. Use Muxus or Ringleader for card advantage so Broadside Bombardiers can finish.

Use multiple targets (Lackey into Chain into Piledriver), or cast threats in response to counters. Use Hexing Squelcher to protect key spells. Sometimes you need to go off at instant speed or use pump spells to overcome counters. If they counter your combo, pivot to aggressive goblin swarms or Scuttling Doom Engine.
Race them. Use Goblin Bomb to deal direct damage. Use Disfigure to remove their ramp creatures. Do not let them stabilize; pressure early and go off as soon as you have a lethal threat.

Use Hexing Squelcher to protect your threats, Disfigure to remove key aggro creatures, and Goblin Bomb to deal damage. You can also go off quickly to win before they kill you. Do not overextend into sweepers; hold back interaction for their key threats.

Apply pressure early, use Disfigure to remove their answers, and go off with multiple threats. Use Hexing Squelcher to protect your combo pieces. Do not let them stabilize; keep the pressure on and go off at instant speed when they show vulnerability.

It's a race. Prioritize card advantage and protection. Use Disfigure to remove their threats, Hexing Squelcher to protect yours, and Scuttling Doom Engine to recur. Track their graveyard and hand size. Win the interaction war by holding up Disfigure for their key threats and going off when they are tapped out.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, counterspells, and graveyard hate.
Note if the opponent is playing around your combo (holding up removal/counters).
Track their mana and hand size to know when to go off.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: These cards are just more ways to put your Goblins in play... Sling-Gang Lieutenant makes your Goblins drain your opponent's life points all at once.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Battle Cry Goblin, Howlsquad Heavy, and Draconautics Engineer can give haste to your horde of Goblins
Sling-Gang Lieutenant makes your Goblins drain your opponent's life points all at once
Muxus, Goblin Grandee, for instance, is the extreme way to do this

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Any of them on turn 1 will change how the deck performs entirely
sometimes you'll have to use its ability to filter lands or unused Aether Vials, and it will be crucial
Stingscourger sends things like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn back to your opponent's hand
Consider creating gigantic boards that you can sacrifice instantly with Sling-Gang Lieutenant so you can kill your opponent on their turn.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: try to go over things like Atraxa, Grand Unifier with Broadside Bombardiers
You have many answers to deal with small threats and Murktide Regent
Chalice of the Void for 1 can disrupt Aether Vial
The One Ring can make winning impossible
Nadu, Winged Wisdom can cost you the game
Stingscourger sends things like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn back to your opponent's hand
even if you're against Force of Will
